'''Karl Temata''' (born 12 July 1978) is a former [[Cook Islands national rugby league team|Cook Islands]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|pr|sr|lf}} or {{rlp|wg}}er in the 2000s and 2010s. He played at club level for the [[Hibiscus Coast Raiders]], [[New Zealand Warriors]], [[London Broncos#1994–2005: Broncos and Super League|London Broncos]]/[[London Broncos#2006–2011: Harlequins Rugby League|Harlequins]] and [[Oxford Rugby League|Oxford]].

==Background==
Temata was born in [[Whangārei]], New Zealand. He is of Māori and Cook Islands descent.

==Playing career==
Temata played for the [[London Broncos#2006–2011: Harlequins Rugby League|Harlequins RL]] in the [[Super League]]. He previously played for the [[New Zealand Warriors]] and the [[Hibiscus Coast Raiders]]. He was named the [[London Broncos#2006–2011: Harlequins Rugby League|Harlequins RL]] player of the year in 2009.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/london/9059969.stm |title=Prop Karl Temata signs Harlequins deal |publisher=BBC Sport |date=4 October 2010 |access-date=31 May 2022}}</ref>

In 2011 Temata signed a one-year extension with Harlequins RL for the 2012 season.<ref>[http://www.rleague.com/content/article.php?id=41318 Harlequins re-sign Karl Temata] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20111014043225/http://rleague.com/content/article.php?id=41318 |date=2011-10-14 }} ''rleague.com'', 25 September 2011</ref>

==Representative career==
Temata played for the [[Cook Islands national rugby league team|Cook Islands]] in the 2000 Rugby League World Cup, and scored a try in the Cook Islands' 38-6 defeat by Wales in Wrexham. In September 2011 he was named in the Cook Islands squad to play New Zealand in Rarotonga on 6 October 2011 ahead of New Zealand's participation in the 2011 Four Nations in England, and Wales.
